prediction.summary: A critical data exfiltration incident has been detected involving the service account 'svc_etl' on host 10.20.3.15. Over 1.1GB of data, identified as containing FINANCE_PII, was uploaded to a suspicious external domain, upload.evil-cdn.net, within a short timeframe. Multiple security systems, including DLP, SIEM, and IDS, confirmed the exfiltration pattern and flagged it as a critical event.

timeline.event: Service account 'svc_etl' on host 10.20.3.15 initiates a connection to upload.evil-cdn.net:443.
Service account 'svc_etl' uploads 524.288 MB to upload.evil-cdn.net/bucket/xfer.
Service account 'svc_etl' uploads an additional 612.352 MB to upload.evil-cdn.net/bucket/xfer.
Firewall detects an outbound anomaly: 1.1GB sent from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44 (upload.evil-cdn.net's IP) in 77 seconds.
DLP detects sensitive label FINANCE_PII in outbound transfer by 'svc_etl'.
SIEM identifies a data exfiltration pattern: large encrypted upload to a rare external domain.
IDS suspects MITRE T1041 Exfiltration Over C2 Channel from host 10.20.3.15.
Proxy logs TLS SNI for upload.evil-cdn.net by client 'svc_etl'.
Netflow detects a volume spike (14x baseline) from 10.20.3.15 to 185.220.101.44:443.
SIEM issues a CRITICAL alert for data_exfiltration by user 'svc_etl' to dest 'upload.evil-cdn.net'.
EDR reports process 'curl' uploading '/data/finance_q2.zip' initiated by 'svc_etl'.

recommendations: Immediately isolate host 10.20.3.15 from the network to prevent further data loss or lateral movement.
Disable or reset credentials for the 'svc_etl' service account and investigate its activity history for other suspicious actions.
Block the malicious domain 'upload.evil-cdn.net' and IP address '185.220.101.44' at all network perimeters (firewall, proxy, DNS).
Perform a full forensic analysis on host 10.20.3.15 to determine the initial compromise vector, identify any other malware, and assess the full scope of exfiltrated data.
Determine the exact contents and sensitivity level of the exfiltrated '/data/finance_q2.zip' file and initiate appropriate data breach response procedures.
Review and strengthen data loss prevention (DLP) policies and controls, especially for sensitive data categories like FINANCE_PII.
